title: Bounded Node Upload Admission and Quarantine · Compiler Case: Filename Normalization
summary: Predict unknown input, typed state, command, stream, promise, storage, cache, security, cost, and recovery outcomes before reviewing Node upload-admission workflow; use streaming request limits, runtime schemas, safe display names, backpressure, cancellation, and explicit quarantine states. Planned artifact: Add and verify the filename normalization increment in Node upload-admission workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Filename Normalization · Theory Filename Normalization
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 9703 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate upload byte and concurrency budgets before a quarantined state transition; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.

Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
